/pub/mesa.help Sep 8, 2006 GL Overview ----------- GL is a set of 3-D graphics functions for use with XWindows and MS Windows. Originally developed by Silicon Graphics, "OpenGL" has become the industry standard version. Open

has become the industry standard version. OpenGL is available for many operating systems, from http://www.opengl.org/. OpenGL on Instructional computers --------------------------------- OpenGL libraries are named "libGL*" and "libglut*" and

GL libraries are named "libGL*" and "libglut*" and are available in 349 Soda (MacOSX) (in /usr/X11R6/lib/) 330 Soda (Windows) Solaris (UNIX) (in /usr/sww/lib, /usr/sww/include/GL, /usr/sww/pkg/Mesa) See http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/cli
pkg/Mesa) See http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/clients for the names of the computers. Instructional Support Group 378/384/386 Cory Hall, 333 Soda inst@eecs.berkeley.edu
